# ICCHAI 2026 — International Conference on Contemplative HealthTech and AI

> A free hybrid academic conference at the intersection of yoga, meditation, AI, and digital health technology.

## What is ICCHAI 2026?

ICCHAI 2026 is the International Conference on Contemplative HealthTech and AI, held October 22–23, 2026 as a free hybrid event. In-person sessions take place at the National Resource Centre for Value Education in Engineering (NRCVEE), IIT Delhi, New Delhi, India. Online attendance is available worldwide at the same times: 18:30–22:30 IST / 9:00 am–1:00 pm EST / 3:00–7:00 pm CET.

## Organizers

- Primary Organiser: YogaXBiofeedback Pvt Ltd (Dhyanly) — https://www.dhyanly.com
- Convening Institution: The India Center, University of Central Florida (UCF) — https://www.ucf.edu
- Institutional Partner: Notre Dame of Maryland University, School of Integrative Health (SOIH)
- Venue and Partner: IIT Delhi, NRCVEE, New Delhi, India

## Conveners

- Satyam Tiwari — Convener, ICCHAI 2026. Founder, YogaXBiofeedback Pvt Ltd (Dhyanly). SRF, IIT Mandi.
- Dr. Steve Haberlin — Co-Convener. Assistant Professor, University of Central Florida.
- Prof. Amy Wheeler-Mantoan — Chair, Academic Programme Committee. Notre Dame of Maryland University.
- Aditya Pandey — Head of Partnerships and Sponsorships India. IIT Mandi.

## Confirmed Speakers

- Shri Akhilesh Mishra — Former Ambassador of India to Ireland and High Commissioner to the Maldives
- Prof. Christopher Key Chapple — Doshi Professor, Loyola Marymount University; Director, MA in Yoga Studies
- Dr. Steve Haberlin — University of Central Florida
- Prof. Amy Wheeler-Mantoan — Notre Dame of Maryland University
- Satyam Tiwari — Founder, YogaXBiofeedback Pvt Ltd (Dhyanly); IIT Mandi
- Prof. James Gomes — Professor, IIT Delhi; Chair, NRCVEE Research Committee
- Dr. Amit Sethi — Director, NERD Lab, University of Utah
- Dr. Rohit Saluja — Assistant Professor, IIT Mandi
- Dr. Kunal Mooley — Assistant Professor, IIT Kanpur

## Six Research Pillars

1. Contemplative Neuroscience and Self-Regulation — EEG, HRV, neuroimaging, pranayama, mindfulness-based interventions
2. Yoga, Ayurveda and Indian Knowledge Systems — yoga therapy, Ayurvedic frameworks, Svara Shastra, classical texts
3. AI and Digital Therapeutics — AI-powered breath recognition, meditation-state classification, generative AI coaching
4. Biofeedback, Wearables and Biosignal Science — HRV wearables, real-time physiological monitoring, biosignal processing
5. Clinical Translation and Digital Health Delivery — telehealth, clinical trial design, integration into healthcare systems
6. Ethics, Responsibility and the Future — data privacy, cultural authenticity, AI transparency, policy frameworks (AYUSH, DST, WHO)

## Programme Format

Each conference day runs four hours in the evening IST (accessible globally). The format includes:
- Opening contemplative practice (breathing or movement)
- Three spotlight talks (20 min each)
- Plenary panel discussion
- Three simultaneous 75-minute breakout tracks (Research / Clinical / Traditional or Technology)
- Book launches or demo sessions
- Closing synthesis or mindfulness practice

## Book Launches at ICCHAI 2026

- "Meta-Meditation for Mental Health Routage" by Dr. Steve Haberlin (Day 1)
- "Sutra to Sensor: HealthTech in IKS" by Satyam Tiwari (Day 1)
- "Applying Therapeutic Yoga in Integrative Health" by Prof. Amy Wheeler-Mantoan and Marlysa Sullivan (Day 2)

## Key Facts

- Dates: October 22–23, 2026
- Format: Hybrid (in-person at IIT Delhi + online worldwide)
- Cost: Free to attend
- Registration: https://icchai.com/register
- Schedule: https://icchai.com/schedule
- Contact: ceodhyanly@gmail.com
- Inaugural edition; intended as an annual conference

## Topics Covered

Contemplative HealthTech, AI in healthcare, yoga therapy, Ayurveda, Indian Knowledge Systems, contemplative neuroscience, EEG, HRV biofeedback, digital therapeutics, mindfulness-based interventions, biosignal science, wearable health technology, responsible AI, AI ethics in mental health, telehealth, Svara Yoga, pranayama science, polyvagal theory, meditation-state classification, generative AI, LLM safety, data privacy.
